title: White House accuses Moonshot AI of distilling Anthropic's Fable to build Kimi K3
subtitle: OSTP director Kratsios says the Chinese lab siphoned Fable 5 outputs and ran restricted Nvidia GB300 chips through Thailand; Treasury raises the prospect of sanctions.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: Michael Kratsios, director of the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy, publicly accused Chinese startup Moonshot AI on July 22 of covertly distilling Anthropic's Fable model to train its Kimi K3 system, which launched on July 16. Kratsios said the administration has evidence Moonshot built an internal platform to run distillation attacks across US frontier models while rotating access methods to avoid detection. He separately alleged the company acquired Nvidia GB300 chips restricted from export to mainland China and deployed them in Thailand to train those models. Treasury Department officials indicated sanctions are on the table.

Distillation — training a smaller model on the outputs of a larger one — is not itself illegal, but doing it against a provider's terms of service, at scale, using fabricated accounts, is what the accusation actually turns on. Anthropic has said Moonshot, DeepSeek and MiniMax together generated more than 16 million interactions against Claude using an estimated 24,000 fake accounts. If Treasury moves on sanctions, it would be the first case of US export-control enforcement applied to distilled model outputs rather than chips or weights — a category the government has been reluctant to name until now.

The accusation lands three weeks after China's Ministry of Commerce classified model weights, training pipelines and inference infrastructure as export-controlled — a mirror move to US restrictions — and days before the August 1 deadline in the White House review of Anthropic's frontier framework. Multiple independent AI researchers have publicly disputed that distillation alone could explain Kimi K3's benchmark performance, arguing the model shows architectural choices that stolen outputs cannot fully account for. The dispute is heading toward the evidence problem all such cases run into: proving distillation from behavioral traces is hard, and the burden falls on whoever wants to sanction.

For learners: this is what enforcement of AI export controls actually looks like — not a customs stop at a port, but an intelligence claim built from usage patterns and account fingerprints. If you work with an API from any frontier lab, the terms of service you clicked through are now the front line of a policy fight. Read them. Understand what 'output data may not be used to train competing models' actually forbids, and what a provider's telemetry can prove. That clause has moved from lawyer boilerplate to national security instrument in about eighteen months.

sources.title: Treasury threatens sanctions after White House claims Moonshot distilled Anthropic's Fable — TechCrunch
